summary: rest-utils performs system reconnaissance and HTTPS exfiltration
details: The package's index.js imports os, https, fs, and child_process and contains multiple https.get calls (lines 378, 396, 448, 466, 513) alongside collection of host identifiers via os.hostname() and os.userInfo() (lines 344-345), with filesystem probing via fs.existsSync. The composition of host/user identity gathering plus child_process plus outbound HTTPS in a package advertised as generic 'rest-utils' fits the system-reconnaissance and exfiltration shape with no legitimate counterpart in a REST utility library. The name pattern (webservices.rest-utils) and behavior are consistent with a typosquat/decoy package whose primary effect is collecting installer host identity and shipping it outbound on require.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
